summary: Returns query results for the specified time based metric
description: Returns query results for the specified time based metric. Endpoint: GET /c/{company_id}/report/time_based. Required parameters: metric, company_id. Optional parameters include filters, limit, sort_by, date_start, date_end, include_deleted_candidates, end_point, include_archived_jobs, and more.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit number of results. Defaults to 30.

params.name: date_start
params.description: Used when date_range is ‘range’. Date from which data should be fetched. Should be in format ‘2022-03-01’.

params.name: date_end
params.description: Used when date_range is ‘range’. Date to which data should be fetched. Should be in format ‘2022-03-01’.

params.name: include_deleted_candidates
params.description: Includes results associated with deleted candidates.

params.name: end_point
params.description: End point used to calculate time difference. Possible values are limited based on provided ‘start_point’ parameter. Can be: candidate_disqualified, candidate_hired, candidate_start_date, job_closed, job_created, job_filled, job_published, requisition_approved, requisition_filled, requisition_sent_for_approval.

params.name: include_archived_jobs
params.description: Includes results associated with archived jobs.

params.name: include_archived_requisitions
params.description: Includes results associated with archived requisitions.

params.name: sort_order
params.description: Sort order. Can be: asc, desc. Defaults to desc.

params.name: start_point
params.description: Start point used to calculate time difference. Can be: candidate_applied, candidate_hired, job_created, job_published, requisition_approved, requisition_created, requisition_sent_for_approval.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number used for pagination, can be used only with the limit option.

params.name: unit_precision
params.description: Attribute used to adjust precision of time calculations. Can be: seconds, minutes. Note: minutes option is deprecated - please use seconds.

params.name: date_range
params.description: Date filter. Can be: range, today, yesterday, this_week, last_week, this_month, last_month, this_quarter, last_quarter, this_year, last_year, last_7_days, last_14_days, last_30_days, last_60_days, last_90_days, last_365_days. If value is range, additional parameters ‘date_start’ and ‘date_end’ must be provided.

example_request:
GET /c/{company_id}/report/time_based

Required params: metric, company_id

Optional params: filters, limit, sort_by, date_start, date_end, include_deleted_candidates, end_point, include_archived_jobs
